title: Lifelong Learning
theme: Growth mindset, reflection, adaptation, and future learning habits

overview.tagline: Invest in your future through smart learning, flexible pathways, and recovery after setbacks.
overview.playerFantasy: Build a future career by spending time, money, and energy on the learning choices that unlock stronger pathways over time.
overview.teacherValue: Targets planning, reflection, resilience, and self-management through evidence-rich gameplay where students justify real pathway and training decisions.

gameplayIdentity.whyItDiffersFromMegatrends: Megatrends is built around future-of-work scenarios. Lifelong Learning should revolve around investment, adaptation, barriers, and long-term pathway growth.
funDrivers: trade-offs between short-term comfort and long-term growth
unlocking alternate routes and better jobs
resource pressure across money, time, and energy

learningObjectives: Explain why lifelong learning matters for career adaptability and future opportunities.
Identify learning habits, mindsets, and behaviours that support long-term employability.
Reflect on strengths, gaps, and next steps in personal learning growth.
Apply planning and self-management strategies to future study, work, and training pathways.

sharedStatEffects.description: Consistent learning habits improve long-term progression and future opportunity.
Students practise adapting to setbacks and continuing forward with a growth mindset.
Planning and self-management help students balance learning demands with wellbeing.
Smart upskilling decisions can improve future income potential.
Ongoing learning helps students stay employable when pathways or workplaces change.
Income grows over time when students invest well in future opportunities.

gameplayLoop.label: Receive a goal
gameplayLoop.description: The student starts a round with a pathway target, learning need, or career milestone.

gameplayLoop.label: Face a barrier
gameplayLoop.description: A roadblock, pressure, or industry change makes the easy route unavailable.

gameplayLoop.label: Choose a learning response
gameplayLoop.description: The student selects from training, mentoring, practice, research, or other growth options.

gameplayLoop.label: Pay the cost
gameplayLoop.description: Choices consume time, money, and energy.

gameplayLoop.label: Play a challenge
gameplayLoop.description: A short interactive task determines how well the choice is applied.

gameplayLoop.label: Bank consequences
gameplayLoop.description: The player gains or loses salary growth, pathway access, skill XP, resilience, and balance.

gameplayLoop.label: Capture evidence
gameplayLoop.description: A short reflection or justification is saved for the teacher dashboard.

currencies.description: Used for courses, transport, tools, and training fees.
Spent across study, work, family responsibilities, and recovery.
Represents capacity and wellbeing. Low energy raises burnout risk.
Shows how strongly the player is moving toward a pathway goal.

incomeModel.progressionRule: Learning decisions do not pay cash directly, but they improve future salary, job security, and promotion access.
exampleTradeOffs: short course costs money now but unlocks stronger pay later
mentor support costs time but improves communication and confidence
ignoring a skill gap protects cash now but blocks future growth

eventDeck.description: A new skill is required quickly for a job, placement, or study opportunity.
The direct route is unavailable, so the player must find an alternative.
A mentor, scholarship, traineeship, or workplace opening appears for a short time.
Money, wellbeing, or family pressure reduces the player's available capacity.
Technology or employer expectations shift, forcing adaptation.

employabilityFocus.whyItFits: Lifelong Learning depends on planning, prioritising, and adjusting learning goals over time.
Students evaluate pathways, learning options, and evidence about their future choices.
Reflection, goal-setting, and learning conversations all require clear communication.

activities.title: What Is Lifelong Learning?
activities.purpose: Introduce lifelong learning as a non-linear, career-building idea connected to changing pathways.

activities.title: Learning to Learn Lab
activities.purpose: Ask students to select the strongest learning strategy for different situations and explain their reasoning.

activities.title: Training and Development Sort
activities.purpose: Require students to classify learning and training as formal, informal, on-the-job, or off-the-job.

activities.title: Pathways to Success
activities.purpose: Challenge students to respond to blocked pathways and build alternative routes to the same goal.

activities.title: Continuing Professional Development
activities.purpose: Show how workers keep their skills current long after entering an occupation.

activities.title: Goal Planning Task
activities.purpose: Capture evidence of planning, prioritising, and identifying next learning steps.

activities.title: Setback Recovery Challenge
activities.purpose: Turn resilience and adjustment into a short, rewarding practice loop.

rounds.title: Your Career Is Not One Straight Line
rounds.gameplayHook: Choose a destination and compare several valid routes to get there.
rounds.primaryTension: fastest route vs most resilient route

rounds.title: Learn How You Learn
rounds.gameplayHook: Solve a skill gap before a deadline using limited time and energy.
rounds.mainReward: stronger learning habits and better future readiness

rounds.title: Invest In Training
rounds.gameplayHook: Buy or choose learning opportunities with different cost, speed, and quality.

rounds.title: Survive A Roadblock
rounds.gameplayHook: Recover from a setback by building Plan B and Plan C.

rounds.title: Stay Employable Over Time
rounds.gameplayHook: Keep a career current through changing industry expectations and CPD choices.
rounds.primaryTension: save resources now vs fall behind later
rounds.mainReward: job security and long-term salary growth

assessmentEvidence.prompt: What is lifelong learning and why is it important for career development?
Which learning strategy is best for the scenario, and why?
Create a pathway plan with at least two alternatives if your first route is blocked.
Explain one learning goal you need for your future pathway and how you will work toward it.
Explain how a worker in a chosen occupation would continue professional development throughout their career.
